cloze_setup: I should [______] my communication [______] to match the local culture.
scenario_text: You are in NYC. People are talking fast and interrupting. How do you respond?

options.text: Match their speed. Be blunt, fast, and get straight to the point.
options.feedback: Perfect. In NYC, 'Slow' is seen as 'Unprepared.' Speed is a sign of respect for their time.

options.text: Ask them to please be more polite and wait their turn to speak.
options.feedback: You'll be eaten alive. They aren't being rude; they're being 'Efficient.' Adapt or fail.

x_ray: The US is a continent, not a country. A 'Power Player' knows which version of 'American' to speak.

title: Influencing Peers
image_description: 3D clay style, sitting at a round table with peers from other departments. You need their help, but you aren't their boss.
strategic_brief: Soft Power. You don't have 'Authority,' so you must use 'Alignment.' Show them how helping you helps *them*.
cloze_setup: Influence is about finding the [______] between our [______].
scenario_text: How do you ask the Marketing Lead for a favor?

options.text: I really need this for my project. Can you just do it quickly?
options.feedback: Transactional and selfish. They have their own work to do.

options.text: I noticed your team is focused on [Goal]. If we do this together, it will make your next launch much smoother.
options.feedback: Excellent. You framed your 'Favor' as an 'Opportunity' for them.

x_ray: In a flat hierarchy, 'Social Capital' is the only currency. Spend it by helping others first.

title: Setting Executive Boundaries
image_description: 3D clay style, it's 8 PM on a Friday. Your boss pings you with a 'non-emergency' question. You are with your family.
strategic_brief: The Respect Boundary. If you always answer at 8 PM, you teach people that your time has no value.
cloze_setup: I should [______] my [______] to avoid burnout and earn respect.
scenario_text: Do you answer the ping immediately?

options.text: Yes, I want them to know I am the hardest worker on the team.
options.feedback: Short-term win, long-term loss. You're setting a standard you can't keep.

options.text: Wait until Monday morning (or Sunday night) and give a high-quality answer then.
options.feedback: Correct. Executives respect people who have a life. It signals you are 'In Demand' and 'Disciplined.'

x_ray: Availability is not the same as Value. A 'Power Player' is available when it matters, not whenever they are called.

title: The Executive Summary
image_description: 3D clay style, a very short email draft on a screen. 3 bullet points. No 'Fluff.'
strategic_brief: The TL;DR Culture. The higher up you go, the less people read. Master the 'Bottom Line Up Front' (BLUF).
cloze_setup: I should put the most [______] information in the [______] sentence.
scenario_text: You are updating the CEO on a project. What is the first sentence?

options.text: We are on track for a June 1st launch with no budget blockers.
options.feedback: Perfect. You answered the only two questions the CEO has in 12 words.

options.text: I wanted to let you know that we've been working really hard and the team is doing great...
options.feedback: Too slow. They stopped reading at 'I wanted to let you know.'

x_ray: In the C-Suite, 'Time' is the most expensive resource. If you save them time, you are an ally.

title: The Grand Finale: The Keynote
image_description: 3D clay style, a massive stage with bright lights. You are holding a microphone, looking out at a huge crowd. You are smiling and confident.
strategic_brief: The Transformation. You aren't 'Learning English' anymore. You are leading a movement. Own the stage.
cloze_setup: I am no longer an [______]; I am a [______].
scenario_text: You finish your speech. The audience is silent, then bursts into applause. What is your final 'Vibe' move?

options.text: Bow your head and walk off quickly because you're embarrassed.
options.feedback: You just apologized for your success. Don't do that. Own the moment.

options.text: Stand tall, smile, say 'Thank you,' and make eye contact with the front row before leaving.
options.feedback: LEGENDARY. You have completed the 90-day map. You are a Cultural Power Player.

x_ray: The journey from Day 1 to Day 90 wasn't about words. It was about the person you became while saying them.
